annotations.label: Chain complex

anchor.snippet: An abstract chain complex is a sequence

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex` (and its `ChainComplex` specialization) is exactly a sequence of objects with differentials satisfying `d ∘ d = 0`.

annotations.label: n-chains and boundary maps

anchor.snippet: are called the boundary maps or differentials

annotations.note: The objects `X i` and differentials `d i j` are the fields of the `HomologicalComplex` structure.

annotations.label: Cycles and boundaries

anchor.snippet: the cycles Z n = Ker d n and the boundaries

annotations.note: Cycles `Ker dₙ` are formalized as `HomologicalComplex.cycles`, but boundaries are not a standalone named subobject (handled implicitly via `toCycles`/homology).

annotations.label: Homology group

anchor.snippet: the factor group of the n -cycles by the n -boundaries

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex.homology i` is the homology object in degree `i`, defined as cycles modulo boundaries via the short complex.

annotations.label: Acyclic / exact sequence

anchor.snippet: A chain complex is called acyclic or an exact sequence

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex.Acyclic` is `∀ i, K.ExactAt i`, i.e. vanishing homology everywhere, matching the acyclic/exact-sequence notion.

annotations.label: Singular and simplicial chains

anchor.snippet: the singular chains C n ( X ) are formal linear combinations of continuous maps

annotations.note: `singularChainComplexFunctor` builds the singular chain complex of a topological space, with simplicial chains underneath via `SSet.chainComplexFunctor`.

annotations.label: Exact sequence

anchor.snippet: is called exact if the image of each homomorphism is equal to the kernel

annotations.note: `ShortComplex.Exact` (and `ComposableArrows.Exact` for longer chains) expresses image equals kernel at each spot.

annotations.label: Short exact sequence

anchor.snippet: where ƒ is a monomorphism and g is an epimorphism

annotations.note: `ShortComplex.ShortExact` packages exactness with `f` mono and `g` epi.

annotations.label: Long exact sequence

anchor.snippet: A long exact sequence is an exact sequence indexed by the natural numbers

annotations.note: Arbitrary-length exact sequences are captured by `ComposableArrows.Exact` and acyclic complexes, but there is no dedicated ℕ-indexed long-exact-sequence object.

annotations.label: Five lemma

anchor.snippet: The five lemma states that, if the rows are exact

annotations.note: This lemma is the five lemma for abelian categories, proving the middle map is an isomorphism given exact rows and iso outer maps.

annotations.label: Snake lemma

anchor.snippet: Then there is an exact sequence relating the kernels and cokernels

annotations.note: `SnakeInput.snake_lemma` proves exactness of the six-term sequence with connecting map `δ`.

annotations.label: Abelian category

anchor.snippet: an abelian category is a category in which morphisms and objects can be added

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.Abelian` is the standard abelian-category typeclass extending preadditive with normal mono/epi, finite products, kernels and cokernels.

annotations.label: Abelian category (concrete characterization)

anchor.snippet: More concretely, a category is abelian if

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Abelian` definition is exactly this concrete characterization: zero object, kernels/cokernels, every mono a kernel and every epi a cokernel.

annotations.label: Right derived functors

anchor.snippet: given by the right derived functors of F

annotations.note: `Functor.rightDerived F n` is defined via injective resolutions and homology of the homotopy category.

annotations.label: Exactness criterion via derived functors

anchor.snippet: F is an exact functor if and only if R 1 F = 0

annotations.note: Derived-functor vanishing lemmas and `rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf` exist, but the exact biconditional 'F exact ⟺ R¹F = 0' is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Ext functor

anchor.snippet: The Ext functor is defined by

annotations.note: `Ext R C n` is defined as a derived functor of the Hom bifunctor in an `R`-linear abelian category.

annotations.label: Ext functor (alternative definition)

anchor.snippet: An alternative definition is given using the functor

annotations.note: The modern `Abelian.Ext` defined via derived-category Hom is the alternative symmetric construction, computable by projective or injective resolutions.

annotations.label: Equivalence of the two Ext constructions

anchor.snippet: These two constructions turn out to yield isomorphic results

annotations.note: The derived-category `Abelian.Ext` is balanced (computable both ways), giving the agreement, but no single iso lemma between the two classical derived-functor constructions was found.

annotations.label: Tor functor

anchor.snippet: its left derived functors L n T are defined

annotations.note: `Tor C n` is defined by left-deriving the tensor bifunctor, matching the left-derived-functor definition.

annotations.label: Spectral sequence

anchor.snippet: A spectral sequence is a choice of a nonnegative integer

annotations.note: `SpectralSequence` packages the pages as homological complexes with isomorphisms from each page's homology to the next.

annotations.label: Induced homomorphism on homology

anchor.snippet: A continuous map of topological spaces gives rise to a homomorphism between their

annotations.note: `singularHomologyFunctor` is functorial in `TopCat`, so a continuous map induces a map on singular homology.

annotations.label: Morphism of chain complexes

anchor.snippet: is a family of homomorphisms of abelian groups

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex.Hom` is a degreewise family of maps commuting with the differentials.

annotations.label: Quasi-isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A morphism F is called a quasi-isomorphism if it induces an isomorphism

annotations.note: `QuasiIso f` holds when `f` induces isomorphisms on homology in all degrees.

annotations.label: Functoriality property

anchor.snippet: have the following functoriality property

annotations.note: `homologyFunctor` makes homology a functor, encoding that it respects composition and identities of chain maps.

annotations.label: Exact triple / short exact sequence of complexes

anchor.snippet: is called an exact triple , or a short exact sequence of complexes

annotations.note: A short exact sequence of complexes is `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in the abelian category `HomologicalComplex C c`.

annotations.label: Zig-zag lemma (long exact sequence in homology)

anchor.snippet: sometimes known as the zig-zag lemma

annotations.note: The connecting map `ShortExact.δ` together with `homology_exact₁/₂/₃` gives the long exact homology sequence of a short exact sequence of complexes.
